hypothesis: Augmenting an OMat24-trained conservative uMLIP with ≤5,000 DFT-D3 host–guest configurations (CO2 and CH4 in 50 chemically diverse MOFs) will reduce its 300 K adsorption enthalpy mean absolute error by at least 50% relative to the unaugmented model while changing its bulk modulus mean absolute error on the OMat24 inorganic validation set by less than 5%.
